sources.description: Web research across 16+ Claude + Obsidian projects

GPS Baseline Analysis / Grader Fleet Deadhead Report (367 days, 1.52M breadcrumbs, 9 graders, 24.9% deadhead baseline). SUPERSEDED 2026-04-16 by Fabric pipeline (2 years, 2.67M breadcrumbs, 31.8% grader / 43.1% fleet).

Road Intelligence Platform Comprehensive Findings (Tier 1/2/3 opportunities, $80K conservative / $150K moderate annual fully-loaded projection). SUPERSEDED 2026-04-16 by Fabric pipeline; revised projection $23K-$44.5K fuel / $57.5K-$111K fully loaded / $40K-$55K full fleet.

GPS Baseline Results from Fabric pipeline (finance_fabric workspace, notebooks 10_bronze_gps + 11_silver_gps_segments + 12_gold_grading_activity). 2 years of data (2024-03-06 to 2026-04-09), 2,666,866 deduplicated breadcrumbs, 5,653 shifts, 38 devices, grader deadhead 31.8%, fleet-wide 43.1%. AUTHORITATIVE baseline.

GPS Pipeline Cowork Session chat summary from old laptop. Captures Fabric medallion notebooks construction (10_bronze_gps, 11_silver_gps_segments, 12_gold_grading_activity), lakehouse upload 1.57 GB, Spark recursiveFileLookup fix, receipt of Shawn's handwritten division maps.
Sent version of the data-requests email to PW&U / GIS distribution. 5 items (haul routes dropped vs the 6-item draft email_data_request.md).

Email reply-chain carrying (1) UFA dyed-diesel lock-in at $1.5667/L, 75% of 2025 May-Sep usage, via Tyler Reiter UFA Picture Butte, and (2) Shawn's division corrections (MG-046 Div 2 not Div 3, MG-053 Div 3 no GPS, MG-042 & MG-052 construction CAT 14M graders no GPS by design).
produced_pages: wiki/sources/UFA Price and Division Correction Thread 2026-04-09.md

sources.description: Cross-validated per-unit L/hr rates from three sources: FuelEconomyMain fill records, JD telematics, CAT telematics. Fleet average ~21 L/hr (pipeline flat 22 L/hr was within 5%). Per-unit rates 18.9 (MG-050) to 23.6 (MG-048). Captures same-day Shawn division corrections.

PRD: Road Intelligence Platform. Master requirements document with 50 user stories across 6 personas (Ryan, Shawn, Operators, Graham, Dust Control Crew, System), full architecture (medallion on Fabric, 08-15 notebooks), 5-year financial projection. From laptop project folder.

Refined Build Plan. Master execution plan with 5 sprints (April 14 through October), 21 variables in 6 tiers, 5-level optimization stack (capital to quality feedback), full depot analysis (7 camps), three-program triangle (grading x dust control x base stabilization). 989 lines. 'START HERE' document per old CLAUDE.md.
produced_pages: wiki/sources/Road Intelligence Platform Refined Build Plan.md

sources.description: Original Build Plan (April 6). Project origin document: Ryan's PSD dashboard question led to full platform scope. PyVRP solver choice, two-level optimization, empirical fuel cost model, Fabric/Databricks architecture. Superseded by Refined Build Plan (April 14).

Financial Rebase memo. Rebases April 6 plan's assumption-based projections against UFA pricing ($1.57/L), Titan GPS odometer hours (786 hrs/unit/yr, 42% lower than assumed), JD and CAT telematics. Revises annual grader fuel $583K down to $375K-$400K. Introduces deadhead-ratio sensitivity table.

GPS Baseline Analysis implementation spec. Defines the script that produced the April 14 PDF: Titan record schema, speed-based activity classification (3/15 km/h thresholds), shift detection, depot landmark names, 7 data quality warnings including MG-049 exclusion rationale.

Old Project CLAUDE.md from laptop Claude Code working directory. Points at REFINED_BUILD_PLAN.md as 'START HERE'. Carries data inventory pointer list, fleet roster (stale Titan labels), Fabric connection details (finance_fabric workspace, F2 capacity, notebooks 08-15).

folders.description: Laptop project folder (660 files): Base Stabilization 2017-2022 invoices + mapping + agreements, Dust Control RFPs 2017-2025 + NSC Minerals + storage tanks, Grading divisional maps (Feb/March 2026 scans) + priority spreadsheets, Route_Optimization_GIS shapefiles, fuel directory, Python project src/tests, output phase1-7, scanned operator maps, PSD drivability records. Tier A markdowns individually registered as sources above; Tier B/C files available by path reference.
